violations.text: Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Establishment must label all spray bottles. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2026

Tongs were observed being stored on an unclean surface (hanging off of trash can). Utensils must be stored on a clean, dry surface to prevent contamination. Tongs were moved to 3-bay sink at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment must apply for a RICFSM with RIDOH. Application was printed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food cilantro with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Cilantro was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

A container of carrots was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Hand sink was cleared at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Molluscan Shellfish clams located in 2 door reach-in did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. PIC informed to keep tags with product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information. The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag was lacking. Establishment is to mark all shellfish tags moving forward. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Beans were placed hot in a plastic container greater than four inches deep. Beans were transferred to shallow metal pans at the time of inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

ROP tilapia was observed thawing with ROP packaging still intact. Fish was observed at 38 DF and completely taken out of package at the time of inspection. Proper thawing methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

The doors to the outside, located in the back kitchen, refrigerator storage room, and front entrance is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine, and 1 door freezer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

The door to the outside, located in the bar area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
Clorox wipes were observed in the hand-washing sink in the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Shellstock located in refrigerator did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Shellstock tags are not maintained for 90 days. Shellstock tags or labels are required to be maintained for 90 calendar days from the date the container is emptied. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Sauces were observed held at 51-55 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, made 1 hour prior, moved to another refrigerator at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Noodles were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. At time of inspection, shrimp was placed hot in the refrigerator in plastic containers greater than four inches deep. PIC moved to shallow containers at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Shrimp was observed thawing in prep sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

The door to the outside, located in the dining room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by live cockroaches in kitchen and basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Tongs were stored on an unclean surface (side of trash can) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024
At time of inspection, ambient of prep unit was 50 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC took unit out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
Basins for 3 bay sinks are lacking drain plugs. Drain plugs are required to properly sanitize dishes. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the food prep area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
